penalties.description: Infringements of prohibited AI practices under Article 5
violationExamples: Deploying social scoring AI systems
Using real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces without authorization
Deploying subliminal or manipulative AI techniques
Exploiting vulnerabilities of specific groups
Untargeted scraping of facial images from internet or CCTV
Emotion inference in workplace or education institutions
Biometric categorization to deduce sensitive attributes
Individual predictive policing based solely on profiling

penalties.description: Non-compliance with requirements for high-risk AI systems
violationExamples: Failure to establish a risk management system (Art. 9)
Non-compliance with data governance requirements (Art. 10)
Inadequate technical documentation (Art. 11)
Failure to maintain automatic logging (Art. 12)
Insufficient transparency and information to deployers (Art. 13)
Failure to enable human oversight (Art. 14)
Not meeting accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity standards (Art. 15)
Failure to implement post-market monitoring (Art. 72)
Not reporting serious incidents (Art. 73)
Failure to maintain technical documentation (Art. 53)
Non-compliance with copyright requirements (Art. 53)
Not providing training data summary (Art. 53)
Failure to provide information to downstream providers (Art. 53)
Non-compliance with systemic risk obligations (Art. 55)

penalties.description: Non-compliance with transparency obligations for limited-risk AI systems
violationExamples: Failure to disclose AI interaction to natural persons (Art. 50)
Not labelling AI-generated synthetic content (Art. 50)
Failure to disclose emotion recognition (Art. 50)
Not labelling deepfakes as AI-generated (Art. 50)

penalties.description: Supplying incorrect, incomplete, or misleading information to notified bodies or authorities
violationExamples: Providing false information during conformity assessment
Misleading notified bodies about system capabilities
Incomplete disclosure of training data or methods
Supplying incorrect documentation to market surveillance authorities

smeReductions.description: Reduced fines for SMEs and startups — the lower of the two amounts (fixed or turnover-based) applies instead of the higher
smeReductions.note: For SMEs including startups, each fine is the lower of the two amounts specified (fixed EUR amount or turnover percentage), rather than the higher amount that applies to larger organizations.

euInstitutionCap.description: EU institutions, agencies, and bodies are subject to fines up to EUR 1,500,000
